question: Will WTI crude oil cost more than $150/barrel before June? (According to investing.com)
reason: WTI crude oil prices are projected to average below $150/barrel before June.
evidence: The U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) forecasts WTI prices to average $62.33/barrel in 2025 and $55.58/barrel in 2026. ([spglobal.com](https://www.spglobal.com/energy/en/news-research/latest-news/crude-oil/061025-crude-price-fall-to-continue-in-2025-26-as-global-production-outpaces-demand-us-eia?utm_source=openai))

A report from CrudeIR indicates that WTI prices are expected to average in the mid-to-upper $90s per barrel through Q3 2026, moderating toward the low-$80s by year-end. ([crudeir.com](https://www.crudeir.com/wti-crude-oil-forecast-2026/?utm_source=openai))
TradersUnion projects WTI prices to average $94.21 in June 2026, with a gradual decline thereafter. ([tradersunion.com](https://tradersunion.com/currencies/forecast/wti-crude-oil/?utm_source=openai))
searchQueries: current WTI crude oil price May 2026
